w:r.w:t: A hardware-accelerated 3×3 grayscale convolution coprocessor on Nexys A7 (Artix-7), driven by a custom RV32I + RV32M 3-stage pipeline with DSP48E1-inferred MAC units and a memory-mapped MMIO coprocessor interface.
Integration stage — Phase 1 (RV32M 8-op extension) complete; Phase 2 coprocessor RTL simulation-verified; full FPGA board-level synthesis pending.
All 8 RV32M ops pass (33/33); conv engine (33/33) and line buffer (29/29) sim-verified; MMIO decoder integrated; CustomTkinter GUI and C software workloads active.
RV32I 3-stage pipeline (IF/ID → EX → WB) with hazard unit: data forwarding, load-use stalls, and branch handling.
RV32M 8-op ALU (MUL, MULH, MULHSU, MULHU, DIV, DIVU, REM, REMU) via rv32m_alu with DSP48E1 inference and 32-cycle iterative divider FSM.
Pipeline stall: pipe_stall = stall_read || div_busy; start gated on ~div_busy to prevent FSM re-trigger; hazard unit exhaustively tested.
3×3 conv engine (conv_engine.v) with DSP48E1 MAC array — 9-tap dot product per output pixel. 33/33 tests passed.
Line buffer (line_buffer.v) with pipelined BRAM — synchronous latency resolved via pipeline register insertion. 29/29 tests passed.
MMIO decoder: kernel regs 0x80000000–0x80000020, START at 0x80000024, STATUS at 0x80000028, NORM at 0x80000030, SW_DONE at 0x80000034.
Extended SW kernel library — multiple filter presets (Sobel, Laplacian, sharpen) selectable at runtime via UART command.
Variable kernel size support (1×1, 3×3, 5×5) switchable in coprocessor without RTL recompilation.
Goals are measurable (see project abstract report)
TBD — functional timing verification pending board bring-up
13,641 LUTs used (21.52% of 63,400) — Vivado synth Apr 19 2026
5,429 FFs used (4.28% of 126,800) — Vivado synth Apr 19 2026
5 RAMB36E1 used (3.70% of 135) — Vivado synth Apr 19 2026
4 DSP48E1 used (1.67% of 240) — Vivado synth Apr 19 2026
Load 8-bit grayscale BMP via UART → BRAM controller → img_bram_in
Sim-verified; GUI sends image bytes over serial
CPU → MMIO → conv engine → poll STATUS until done
MMIO integrated; sw_gaussian_blur.c runs on pipeline
UART TX → GUI display + save to PNG
CustomTkinter GUI ready; output save to PNG implemented
Implement and be ready with at least one core functionality by Apr 12.
Complete the full minimum system by Apr 19, leaving buffer for last-minute surprises.
For Mar 22 – Apr 12: update based on actual execution
For after Apr 12: fill as planned schedule
FSM gates start on ~div_busy; re-trigger loop fixed; 33/33 tests pass.
Kernel write lockout missing — kernel regs writable while conv engine is mid-run.
Gate kernel reg writes on ~conv_busy in mmio_decoder; enforce all 9 writes before asserting START.
Doc lists START=0x80000028; RTL uses 0x80000024. Confirm final addresses before demo.
Run Vivado implementation before Apr 25 to get resource report and confirm timing closure.
Infinite software freeze during hardware polling (HW_DONE)
Implement a maximum cycle timeout in the C polling loop while(HW_DONE_REG == 0) to prevent hard-locking the CPU if hardware faults.
Verify C-loop indices physically map to 0..15875 to prevent overwriting neighboring hardware MMIO registers.
Vivado stale cache loading / zombie .hex files
Bypass Vivado auto-import by hardcoding absolute file paths ($readmemh) directly in instr_mem.v.
Combinatorial loop in MMIO read path causing X-states / Synthesis hangs
Added 1-cycle latency flip-flop (is_mmio_read_wb) to memory mux to properly break the loop and align with WB stage.
rv32m_alu.v — all 8 RV32M ops with DSP48E1 inference and 32-cycle iterative divider FSM.
tb_rv32m_alu.v — stress-test all 8 ops incl. div-by-zero, INT_MIN/−1; 33/33 passing.
hazard_unit.v (fwd + stall) and decoder_ext.v (RV32M decode) — base CPU upgrades.
Integration Lead — Merged Coprocessor/FSM branches into pipeline.v and top_fpga.v. Managed hardware-software boundary and Vivado synthesis workflow.
System Debugging — Resolved critical MMIO datapath bugs (combinatorial loops in read mux) and fixed DMEM Byte vs. Word addressing translation (>> 2).
conv_engine.v (33/33 tests), line_buffer.v (29/29 tests), img_bram_in/out.v — Phase 2 RTL.
mmio_decoder.v and conv_datapath.v — Memory mapping and datapath wiring. top_fsm.v state logic.
UART controller, bare-metal C benchmark codes (sw_blur.c), & CustomTkinter Python GUI.
b163f42 — Merge Sukhjot’s fixes into shaurya branch
Final integration: imported working RTL from Sukhjot’s branch, resolved 28 file conflicts prioritizing functional fixes, added Windows Makefile compatibility.
Full Phase 2 integration: fixed line_buffer BRAM latency, wired top_fsm drain, brought in UART controller and C workloads; 131/131 tests passing post-merge.
2965104 — Full MMIO decoder for kernel regfile + status reads
Maps kernel weights to 0x80000000–0x80000020, START to 0x80000024, STATUS to 0x80000028; adds SW_DONE doorbell for software-triggered TX.
87ff069 — Add CustomTkinter GUI for FPGA coprocessor
Premium dark-mode GUI: sends 128×128 image over UART, reads 126×126 convolved output, displays side-by-side comparison, saves convolved PNG to disk.
Initial rv32m_alu.v: 8-op RV32M ALU with single-cycle multiply (DSP48E1) and 32-cycle iterative restoring divider FSM.
Renamed sw_sobel.c → sw_gaussian_blur.c; updated Python GUI to save convolved output as timestamped PNG.
TA feedback to be added after Apr 25–26 final demo review.
Changes will be documented post-demo based on TA evaluation.
